results.possibleIdentity: Gomi, Y. was a Japanese engineer and engineering consultant active in the late 1960s and early 1970s. Based in Kawasaki City, Kanagawa, Japan, Gomi was professionally affiliated with the H.R. Engineering Consultant Company. During this period, Gomi was a member or technical collaborator of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded by Billy Klüver and Robert Rauschenberg to foster collaborations between artists, engineers, and scientists. Gomi's professional background and contact information were documented in the E.A.T. membership records and index cards, which cataloged technical experts willing to provide consultation and assistance to artists on interdisciplinary projects, such as those associated with the Pepsi Pavilion at Expo '70 in Osaka.
